headline: Great teams,
great
games.
blocks.subhead: Studiotypes are a specialist recruitment partner for the games industry, embedding talent inside studios rather than just filling roles. They asked for a brand and a site with the same confidence they bring to hiring.

blocks.label: The brief
heading: Recruitment,
reframed.
paragraphs: Studiotypes aren't a games studio, they're the people games studios call when a team needs to grow. Campaign Mode covers a single key hire, Coop Mode embeds a recruiter inside the studio full time. The brand needed to read as an insider, not an agency: someone who understands the craft well enough to hire for it properly.

blocks.label: The idea
heading: Teams, built
like blueprints.
paragraphs: The idea came from the studios themselves. Every game starts on a console or a controller in someone's hands, so I built 3D wireframe models of them in Blender, then let those wireframes merge into one another. Two meshes becoming one reads as two people, two skill sets, coming together, the studio's foundation taking shape the same way a game's does: piece by piece, team by team.

Whitespace does a lot of the talking. Generous, unhurried layouts give the brand a calm authority, the confidence of a partner who doesn't need to shout. Warm gradients and precise, restrained typesetting keep it feeling current without ever tipping into noise.

blocks.text: Early exploration stayed rough on purpose, wireframe sketches, scribbled notes on pacing and scroll behaviour, a header tried a dozen ways before it earned its size. The controller mesh took shape here first, long before it found its glow.

blocks.caption: The controller, modelled and lit as the site's opening line.

blocks.label: The result
heading: Open world
hiring.
paragraphs: The finished site carries that same confidence through, gaming's own language borrowed straight-faced: Campaign Mode, Coop Mode, “open world hiring.” Restraint turned out to be the brand's best asset. It reads like a studio talking to other studios, because that's exactly what it is.

items.value: Brand identity, web design, art direction
